# Chapter 3 Navigating Machine Accounting

The IGT Spade provides menu access to functions within the MA system. Some of these
functions are also accessed in other locations.

Customize the IGT Machine Accounting System by selecting options for the Quick Access
toolbar, or use the default view.

Use the Select Tasks Groups option to choose the available tasks appearing on the User Tasks
list and the Period Timeline Ribbon. Configure these items based on each auditor's duties.
Group Tasks lists all items in the group that must be processed to complete the entire task.


## 3.1 IGT Spade

To access the IGT Spade:

Click the IGT Spade in the upper-left corner of the window. The IGT Spade menu expands.




![Figure 3-1 IGT Spade Options](MachineAccounting_UserGuide_images/25.1.png)




<!-- PageFooter="Machine Accounting 9.7.5" -->
<!-- PageNumber="25" -->
<!-- PageBreak -->

<!-- PageHeader="Chapter 3 Navigating Machine Accounting" -->

<td>Open Period</td>
<td>Select a different period to process or start a new period.</td>

<tr>

<tr>

<tr>
<td rowspan="2"></td>

<tr>
<td>· EGM Enable and Disable-Enable or disable individual machines or groups of machines on the casino floor on a preset schedule.</td>

<td>· Floor Staff Information-View and manage floor staff records. Establish equipment types and maintain equipment custody lists.</td>
</tr>

<td>· Equipment-Add, modify, and delete floor equipment in the system.</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>· Equipment Types-Add, modify, and delete floor equipment types.</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>· Pagers-Define, modify, or delete pager zones and triggers.</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td rowspan="6">Configuration</td>
<td>· User Management-View and manage all system users. · Change Password-Opens the Change Password window for users to change their own password.</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>· Modify User Tasks-Modify user tasks.</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>· Player ID Locales-Add and delete country and state abbreviations used in Machine Accounting.</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>· Configure Flash Report Tasks-Create reports run as a user task during the audit process. · Define Accounting Calendar-Defines dates for end-of-month configuration.</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>· Configure Lease Machines-Defines lease settings to groups of machines.</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>· Configure Progressive Workbook-Add, edit, or delete progressive workbook items.</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td rowspan="5">View</td>
<td>· User Tasks-Opens user task list. · Reports-Opens list of reports available in the system.</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>O Ž Preliminary reports are available at any time during the period. Final reports for an accounting period are only available after the period is closed.</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>· Status Bar-Displays the current user (located along the bottom of the Machine Accounting window). Toggle the Status Bar to On or Off.</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>· Navigation Panes-Toggle the User Tasks list and Reports list panes to On or Off.</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>· Reset Pane Layout-Reset navigation pane layout to default view.</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Options</td>
<td>Configure system settings and settings for the accounting period, and set all levels of mandatory and voluntary tax rates.</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Print Setup</td>
<td>Setup printer and print options for Machine Accounting reports.</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Help</td>
<td>Display program information, product version, and copyright.</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Exit</td>
<td>Close the Machine Accounting application.</td>
</tr>
</table>

## 3.2 Quick Access Toolbar

The Quick Access Toolbar is used to access commonly used functions and can be customized to
add and remove functions, move functions to other locations within the MA window, and be
reset to the default, if needed. Hovering the mouse pointer over a toolbar option displays a tool
tip with the function or workbook name, when enabled.




![Figure 3-2 Quick Access Toolbar](MachineAccounting_UserGuide_images/27.1.png)




### 3.2.1 Changing Its Location

The Quick Access Toolbar can be relocated to meet user needs.

To change the location of the Quick Access Toolbar:

1\. Click Customize Quick Access Toolbar. The Customize Quick Access Toolbar menu
expands.

2\. In the list, select one of the following from the drop-down list:

· Show Below the Ribbon

· Show Above the Ribbon




![](MachineAccounting_UserGuide_images/27.2.png)




3\. Click More Commands. The Customize window appears.




![](MachineAccounting_UserGuide_images/27.3.png)




<!-- PageFooter="Machine Accounting 9.7.5" -->
<!-- PageNumber="27" -->
<!-- PageBreak -->

<!-- PageHeader="Chapter 3 Navigating Machine Accounting" -->

4\. Select one of the following:

· Place Quick Access Toolbar below the Ribbon

· Place Quick Access Toolbar above the Ribbon


### 3.2.2 Adding Commands

To add a command to the Quick Access Toolbar:

1\. Click Customize Quick Access Toolbar. The Customize Quick Access Toolbar menu
expands.

2\. Select More Commands. The Customize window appears.

3\. Select a command in the left pane.

4\. Click Add >>. The command appears on the right pane. Repeat for all desired commands.

5\. Click Close. The Quick Access Toolbar updates with the selected commands.


### 3.2.3 Removing Commands

To remove a command from the Quick Access Toolbar:

1\. Click Customize Quick Access Toolbar. The Customize Quick Access Toolbar menu
expands.

2\. Select More Commands. The Customize window appears.

3\. Select a command in the right box.

4\. Click Remove. The command no longer appears on the right pane. Repeat for all desired
commands.

5\. Click Close. The Quick Access Toolbar updates with the selected commands.


### 3.2.4 Resetting Defaults

To reset the Quick Access Toolbar to default settings:

1\. Click Customize Quick Access Toolbar. The Customize Quick Access Toolbar menu
expands.

2\. Select More Commands. The Customize window appears.

3\. Click Reset. A warning message appears.

4\. Click Yes. The Quick Access Toolbar resets to the default view.


### 3.2.5 Customizing Keystrokes

Keystrokes can be assigned to frequently used tasks to further customize the application.

To customize a keystroke:

1\. Click Customize Quick Access Toolbar. The Customize Quick Access Toolbar menu
expands.

2\. Select More Commands. The Customize window appears.

3\. Click the Keyboard tab. The Keyboard tab appears.

<!-- PageFooter="Machine Accounting 9.7.5" -->
<!-- PageNumber="28" -->
<!-- PageBreak -->

<!-- PageHeader="Chapter 3 Navigating Machine Accounting" -->




![](MachineAccounting_UserGuide_images/29.1.png)




4\. Select a command from the Commands pane.

5\. Press the new shortcut key(s) on the keyboard. The information appears in the Press new
shortcut key field.

6\. Click Assign. The shortcut key(s) bind to the selected command.

7\. Repeat as needed, then click Close.


### 3.2.6 Additional Options

Additional options are available in the Customize window to configure the toolbar in other
ways.

To customize additional options:

1\. Click Customize Quick Access Toolbar. The Customize Quick Access Toolbar menu
expands.

2\. Select More Commands. The Customize window appears.

3\. Click the Options tab. The Options tab appears.

4\. Select the desired options.

5\. Click Close. The selected options take effect.

<!-- PageFooter="Machine Accounting 9.7.5" -->
<!-- PageNumber="29" -->
<!-- PageBreak -->

<!-- PageHeader="Chapter 3 Navigating Machine Accounting" -->




![](MachineAccounting_UserGuide_images/30.1.png)




## 3.3 Customizing the Interface




![](MachineAccounting_UserGuide_images/30.2.png)




The Select Task Groups is configured for each user with Audit Workbook
permission.

To access the Select Task Group window:

Navigate to IGT Spade > Configuration > Modify User Tasks. The Select Task Groups
window appears.




![Figure 3-3 Select Task Groups](MachineAccounting_UserGuide_images/30.3.png)






![](MachineAccounting_UserGuide_images/30.4.png)




All tasks must be completed in order to close the audit period, regardless of
whether or not the task displays in the panel.

<!-- PageFooter="Machine Accounting 9.7.5" -->
<!-- PageNumber="30" -->
<!-- PageBreak -->

<!-- PageHeader="Chapter 3 Navigating Machine Accounting" -->

To customize the User Tasks list:

1\. Navigate to IGT Spade > Configuration > Modify User Tasks. The Select Task
Groups window appears.

2\. Use the Groups Available and Groups Selected panes, along with the arrows, to move
items between Groups Available and Groups Selected.

3\. Use the Up and Down buttons to reorder the items within the groups.




![](MachineAccounting_UserGuide_images/31.1.png)




Ť
Click Reset Order at any time to undo any changes and return to the list's
original order.

4\. Click Apply to set the changes.

5\. Click OK to finish. The User Tasks list appears with the new changes.
